item.title: Japan’s Katayama Says Always Ready to Take Steps on Currencies as Needed
item.description: Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said authorities are always ready to take steps as needed on currencies — days after the release of data showing that authorities conducted a record spate of currency intervention in the period between late April and late May.

item.title: Congo Reopens Main Airport in Ebola Epicenter as Officials Brace for Long Outbreak
item.description: Congo reopened the main airport in the eastern province hardest hit by Ebola, after health officials reported tentative signs the outbreak may be slowing despite a continuing struggle to trace exposed contacts and investigate suspected cases.

item.title: Optimum Pushes Apollo, Ares Debt Feud to Brink With Asset Shift
item.description: One of the most bitter feuds in distressed-debt investing, between billionaire Patrick Drahi and some of the world’s biggest investors, has suddenly gotten even more contentious.

item.title: South Korea Overtakes India as World’s Sixth-Largest Stock Market
item.description: South Korea’s equity market has overtaken India’s as the world’s sixth largest, driven by a relentless surge in chip heavyweights powering the global artificial intelligence buildout.

item.title: Chinese Curbs Risk Derailing Record Hong Kong Home-Buying Spree
item.description: Chinese buyers who spent more than ever on Hong Kong real estate in the first quarter now face a hurdle from tighter rules on wealthy people in the mainland taking cash overseas.

item.title: Short Seller Andrew Left Found Guilty of Securities Fraud
item.description: Andrew Left, one of the world’s most prominent short sellers, was found guilty of securities fraud by a federal jury after a landmark trial that scrutinized his use of social media to move the price of stocks.

item.title: Bloom CEO Has No Plans to Sell Shares After AI Investor Run-up
item.description: Bloom Energy Corp., a supplier to Oracle Corp. that’s seen its stock price double in the past two months, doesn’t see a need to sell shares to meet surging demand from data centers, its chief executive officer says.

item.title: Swiss Dental Startup vVardis Is Preparing for US IPO
item.description: vVardis Holding AG, a Swiss dental startup, is working with banks for a US initial public offering that could come this year, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Gold Holds Decline as Traders Weigh Confusion Over US-Iran Talks
item.description: Gold held a decline as conflicting signals from the US and Iran cast doubt over a diplomatic resolution to the war, fanning concerns over inflation and prolonged trade disruptions.

item.title: S. Korea’s Inflation Accelerates, Supporting BOK’s Hawkish Tilt
item.description: South Korea’s consumer inflation accelerated to the fastest pace in more than two years, reinforcing the central bank’s tilt toward higher interest rates as the impact from the Middle East conflict ripples across the economy.

item.title: China Robotics Firms Line Up IPOs to Pitch Next Phase of AI
item.description: China is pitching itself as the global fulcrum for the next phase of artificial intelligence and a legion of robotics companies is lining up initial public offerings to test investor appetite.

item.title: Traders Turn to AI to Crack Secret Formula Behind PBOC’s FX Fix
item.description: It’s been a source of intrigue and confusion among currency traders for years. Just how does China decide each day where it’s going to set its fixing rate for the yuan — a reference point that determines its permitted trading range for the next session?

item.title: S&P 500 Futures Dip With No Iran Deal, Oil Steady: Markets Wrap
item.description: US equity-index futures edged lower and oil held gains as efforts to revive a peace deal with Iran showed little progress.

item.title: Oil Holds Biggest Gain in a Month on Standoff in US-Iran Talks
item.description: Oil steadied after its biggest gain in about a month, as uncertainty about the state of US-Iran peace talks raised the risk that energy flows from the Persian Gulf could be curtailed for longer.

item.title: Australian Wheat Crop Expected to Drop 26% on Weather, War Costs
item.description: Australia’s winter wheat crop is forecast to fall by more than a quarter in the 2026/27 season due to unusually dry weather, low global prices and higher input costs resulting from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Anthropic’s First-Mover IPO Edge Set to Widen Lead Over OpenAI
item.description: Anthropic PBC pulled ahead of OpenAI with its confidential IPO filing Monday, as the free-spending artificial intelligence startups battle for a fundraising edge that’s set to determine who will win the ultimate battle for computing power.

item.title: Elliott Takes Stake in Miner Northern Star, Pushing for Review
item.description: Elliott Investment Management LP has built a large stake in Northern Star Resources Ltd. and is pushing for a strategic review and leadership changes at the Australian mining company.

item.title: CFTC Awards Five Whistleblowers $8 Million for Enforcement Tips
item.description: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission said it will award more than $8 million to five whistleblowers who provided information that helped agency investigators recover funds for defrauded investors.

item.title: Nvidia’s AI Chips Sought by Chinese Labs With Ties to Military
item.description: At least seven Chinese universities that support the country’s armed forces and defense industry are seeking access to Nvidia Corp.’s H200 chips, the most powerful artificial intelligence processors ever allowed by the US to be sold in China, according to a review of procurement records.

item.title: Can the OpenAI and Anthropic IPOs Live Up to Expectations?
item.description: The AI dynamos are eyeing the public markets, but some wonder whether SpaceX’s planned June listing will steal their thunder.

item.title: Japan Faces Shortage of Bananas as Middle East Impact Spreads
item.description: Japan is slipping toward a banana shortage crisis, the latest disruption linked to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Mach Industries Valued at $1.8 Billion in Latest Funding Deal
item.description: Defense startup Mach Industries notched a $1.8 billion valuation in a funding deal to ramp up production of its autonomous aircraft, strike systems and other gear for the Pentagon and allied forces.

item.title: Alphabet to Raise $80 Billion in Equity for AI Spending
item.description: Google parent Alphabet Inc. is raising $80 billion in equity offerings, including an investment deal with Berkshire Hathaway Inc., to help fund its ambitious artificial intelligence spending plans.

item.title: Canada Touts US Oil Trade Before Minister’s Talks With Greer
item.description: The office of Canada-US Trade Minister Dominic LeBlanc stressed the importance of “maintaining the highly integrated North American energy market” ahead of talks in Washington with US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer.

item.title: Vast Space Expands Into Europe With French Astronaut Missions
item.description: Vast Space, founded by former crypto tycoon Jed McCaleb, will establish a European headquarters in Paris and send French astronauts on two crewed missions to space, the company announced Monday.

item.title: Chile President Lays Out Security Agenda and Vandal Black List
item.description: Chile President José Antonio Kast said in his first State of the Nation address that he’ll roll out new public security measures in coming weeks, including one to create a black list of “vandals” who would lose the right to some social benefits.

item.title: HPE Sees Strong Growth Over Next 18 Months on Server Demand
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co. gave an outlook for annual sales that topped estimates, citing massive growth in demand for the company’s servers and networking that it expects to continue through next year. The shares soared in extended trading.

item.title: GoPro Warns of Going-Concern Risk Amid AI-Fueled Memory Crunch
item.description: Surging memory costs are pushing action-camera maker GoPro Inc. to the brink. The company, founded by Nicholas Woodman, warned of risks to its ability to continue as a going concern and is seeking financing to avert a default, according to a latest filing.

item.title: BMO Hires TD’s van Arragon for Domestic Business Banking Role
item.description: Bank of Montreal hired Trevor van Arragon from rival Toronto-Dominion Bank for a newly created role leading BMO’s Canadian business banking division.

item.title: M&A Lawyer Pleads Not Guilty to Leading Insider-Trading Ring
item.description: A former mergers and acquisitions lawyer with top firms including Latham & Watkins and Goodwin Procter pleaded not guilty to charges that he led a massive insider trading ring that made tens of millions of dollars in illegal profits.
